Security Alert: U.S. Embassy Jordan - July 9, 2026

Location: Jordan, countrywide 

Event: Reports indicate missiles, drones, or rockets are in Jordanian airspace. Seek overhead cover and shelter in place immediately. Remain indoors and pay attention to local announcements and alerts.

The U.S. Embassy in Jordan will continue to review the situation and provide additional information as needed. 

Assistance: 

· U.S. Embassy – Amman, Jordan 

· Embassy address: Al-Umawyeen 37, Amman, Jordan 

· Phone number (emergencies during business hours): +962 6 590-6000 

· Phone number (emergencies after business hours): +962 6 590-6500 · Contact the U.S. Citizen Services Unit · Embassy website: https://t.co/v78o1nLZvN Homepage - U.S. Embassy in Jordan

· State Department – Consular Affairs: 888-407-4747 or 202-501-4444

· Jordan Country Information

· Enroll in the Smart Traveler Enrollment Program (STEP) to receive security updates 

· Follow the Department of State on Facebook and X/Twitter.

Actions to Take:  

· Monitor local media, such as Roya News, Petra News and Jordan Times for information. 

· Contact your airline directly for flight change details if your flight is affected. 

· Be aware of your surroundings. 

· Remain vigilant for anything appearing out of the ordinary. 

· Contact the Jordanian police by dialing 911 if you are in immediate danger.

HORMUZ TRAFFIC DROPS AS CEASEFIRE COLLAPSES

Ship crossings through the Strait of Hormuz fell to 25 yesterday, below the recent daily average of 30–50.

Traffic slowed after President Trump declared the U.S.–Iran ceasefire over and both sides exchanged further strikes.

Maritime analysts say the recent recovery in shipping activity has collapsed, with the Strait again operating under full conflict conditions.

Vessels may continue to increase transit capacity provided they adhere to security protocols and receive authorization from the IRGC Navy.

The IRGC Navy maintains that foreign entities have no role in the region and warned that further interference in maritime navigation will be met with a response from Iranian forces.

quoted.text:
Quick copper thought - Bernstein is balancing macro pressure with a tight physical market.

Higher energy costs, stronger dollar and rate expectations can pressure commodities short term. But copper still has support from reduced mine production guidance and U.S. inventory stockpiling.

That is the part I care about for $NRED.

If copper supply stays tight into the back half of the decade, companies with serious exploration angles become more relevant. NovaRed brings Wilmac, BC copper-gold-platinum potential, and MetalCore AI for better target generation.

Copper demand is loud. Supply is the bottleneck.

quoted.text: Since the Strait "opened" a surge of >80MM barrels exited heading for port. That is now over, with all but 1 of the 109 non-Iranian tankers having exited. What to watch now? INBOUND traffic, with only 1 vessel entering last night vs. a norm of ~50 and 7 day avg. only ~20% of normal. Without normalized inbound traffic onshore storage will remain filled and production constrained by ~5-8MM Bbl/d as Middle Eastern exports in July remain down 5.6MM Bbl/d, even with Fujairah/Yanbu workarounds. With the US SPR ~19MM bbls above minimum operating levels, global SPRs greatly reduced, refined product stocks at historic lows (ie. record high crack spreads), onshore crude inventories near record lows, and China emerging from hibernation, the oil market today is much more vulnerable to a price spike than it was several months ago.
quoted.author: ericnuttall

quoted.text:
JUST IN: A source close to Iranian officials says Tehran is deliberately holding back its full military firepower while tightening control over the Strait of Hormuz — because time and oil are on Iran's side, per some Iranian outlets.

The source explained that Trump's window to avoid a global economic crisis is shrinking fast. The U.S. Strategic Petroleum Reserve is now just 19 million barrels above its minimum operating level — the cushion the U.S. has been using to keep oil prices artificially low amid escalating tensions. At current drawdown rates, that floor could be hit by early August. Meanwhile, Iran is exporting oil freely with the blockade lifted, steadily draining the U.S. safety net while Tehran builds its leverage.

The source also confirmed that Iran has adopted a new doctrine: any fresh U.S. strike will trigger the immediate and complete closure of the Strait of Hormuz, with retaliation at a minimum 2:1 ratio. The Strait is one decision away from shutdown, and the SPR safety net is nearly gone. Trump is escalating while the U.S. has no cushion left — and if the Strait closes, $150+ oil is not unrealistic.

He emphasized that the chokepoint remains the ultimate leverage — and Tehran knows it.
quoted.author: HormuzReport

quoted.text:
Hey resource people, Bernstein’s copper outlook is basically saying: short-term noise, long-term squeeze.

They see copper around $12,419 per ton in 2026, while structural supply deficits are expected to emerge later this decade. That is the key line for me. The world can argue about rates and the dollar, but mine supply is getting tighter.

That is why $NRED stays on my screen. Wilmac gives NovaRed 16,078 hectares in British Columbia near Copper Mountain, and MetalCore AI gives them a smarter way to process geological, geochem and historical exploration data.

When copper supply gets constrained, early target generation matters more.
